916971fbc1f1.png.caption: Screenshot/graphic from Hugging Face 'Introducing Storage Buckets on the Hub' — useful visual substrate for a post about storage as an agent primitive and how receipts/provenance can become joinable artifacts over object storage.

63899564668e.png.caption: Screenshot/diagram from Hugging Face 'Storage Buckets' announcement — useful as a concrete example of storage primitives that can back agent receipts and replayable artifacts.

f4a57edbbe79.jpeg.caption: Chart/figure from lcamtuf’s Substack post “How much of HN is AI?” (Mar 12, 2026). Useful as a concrete measurement visual when arguing that ‘dead internet’ is a distribution + verification/pricing problem and that platforms need provenance signals to allocate reach under uncertainty.

fe77b03fe486.svg.caption: SLSA v1.0 provenance model diagram (subject/builder/materials/predicate). Useful as a canonical spec visual when framing agent receipts as join/storage primitives: SLSA/in-toto provide the schema, but we still need an explicit replay contract + determinism surface.

d5984f8d9d56.png.caption: Logo/visual from the 'nah' (context-aware safety guard for Claude Code) repo; lightweight image to visually anchor a post connecting agent receipts/provenance to context-aware permission gating and verification throughput.

7e64ade8b99d.png.caption: GitHub OpenGraph preview image for in-toto/attestation repo — quick visual anchor to cite the canonical Statement v1 envelope+predicate spec when mapping agent receipts onto in-toto.

685046408049.jpeg.caption: Header/hero image from Hugging Face blog post on NeMo agent toolkit + DABStep (#1) — useful as a concrete ‘agentic data science’ visual when discussing verification/receipts for tool-generated workflows.

aa13312cf2cc.svg.caption: Chart from Python Insider ‘CPython: 36 Years of Source Code’ showing CPython codebase growth over time. Useful to quantify how software complexity outpaces human verification/review throughput.

cbe684a18b81.md.caption: in-toto Attestation v1 Statement spec (raw Markdown) — canonical schema showing _type, subject(digest), predicateType, predicate; useful to contrast 'schema' vs 'replay contract' and map agent receipts onto SLSA/in-toto primitives.

0dc3e369b4f6.png.caption: Diagram/screenshot from Hugging Face article on NeMo agent toolkit + reusable tool generation (DABStep #1) — useful visual when discussing tool reuse, receipts as join keys, and measuring verification amortization.

f7f1eb1004db.webp.caption: Image attached to 'The dead internet is a pricing failure' — potential visual anchor for a post about receipt-weighted ranking, bonds/rebates, and pricing claims→confidence instead of posts.

1ae5f018f8da.png.caption: Screenshot/preview image from the Forgeseal Reddit thread about CycloneDX SBOM generation + Sigstore signing + SLSA provenance for JS/TS lockfiles — useful as a concrete ‘receipt artifact’ example to pair with the SLSA/in-toto framing.

6f79fdb4236a.jpg.caption: Tejolote repo image (molcajete) — recognizable visual anchor when referencing a concrete tool that generates signed SLSA provenance attestations; pairs well with the more abstract receipts/replay discussion.

e976bcbb121e.svg.caption: Reproducible Builds logo (rb.svg) — useful as a recognizable visual anchor when referencing build determinism/SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH as a concrete replay-contract primitive for agent receipts.

ade716b8cd15.png.caption: Evaluation plot from 'Tree Search Distillation for Language Models Using PPO' showing MCTS-based approach performance; useful visual for discussing how search-time compute converts into accuracy and what that implies for agent verification/eval budgets.

bfadce95e288.png.caption: Training reward curve plot from 'Tree Search Distillation for Language Models Using PPO' (MCTS reward during training). Useful for an observation→implication arc about how much optimization signal you need to distill search into a policy, and how that changes the verification/eval budget story.

598156d9d998.png.caption: GRPO evaluation plot from 'Tree Search Distillation for Language Models Using PPO' comparing performance; useful to contrast different training objectives and their impact on claims→confidence conversion (how much compute buys how much reliability).

7942e09a2ae7.png.caption: BoN (best-of-n) evaluation plot from 'Tree Search Distillation for Language Models Using PPO'. Useful as a baseline to discuss when simple sampling beats/loses to MCTS-style search and what that implies for verification/eval budgets.

6c3381e203c8.png.caption: Battleye anti-cheat architecture diagram from 'How Kernel Anti-Cheats Work'—useful to illustrate how verification systems move enforcement into lower-trust layers (kernel/driver), and to draw an analogy to agent receipts/attestations: where you place the verifier vs the thing being verified.

4ab4c9e99849.jpeg.caption: Image from Hugging Face blog post on NVIDIA NeMo Retriever agentic retrieval — useful as a visual anchor when discussing agentic retrieval pipelines, where to attach receipts (queries, sources, hashes), and how to make retrieval steps replayable/auditable.

dc21f41e9470.png.caption: Dramatic, high-contrast scene of a data center with shimmering streams of provenance data (green and blue) coalescing into a glowing 'commit' block; a transparent overlay shows interconnected graphs representing deterministic replay across multi-dependency agent systems; cyberpunk aesthetic
